heading: Acceptance of contributions in support of Australia, United Kingdom, and United States submarine security activities

children.heading: Acceptance authority
children.label: a Acceptance authority
children.text: The President may accept from the Government of Australia contributions of money made by the Government of Australia for use by the Department of Defense in support of non-nuclear related aspects of submarine security activities between Australia, the United Kingdom, and the United States (in this section referred to as the “AUKUS partnership”).

children.heading: Establishment of Submarine Security Activities Account
children.label: b Establishment of Submarine Security Activities Account

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a special account to be known as the ‘Submarine Security Activities Account’.

children.heading: Credit to account
children.label: 2 Credit to account
children.text: Contributions of money accepted by the President under subsection (a) shall be credited to the Submarine Security Activities Account.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 3 Availability
children.text: Amounts credited to the Submarine Security Activities Account shall remain available until expended.

children.label: A
children.text: for any purpose authorized by law that the President determines would support the AUKUS submarine security activities;

children.label: B
children.text: to carry out a military construction project that is consistent with the purposes for which the contributions were made and is authorized by law;

children.label: C
children.text: to develop and increase the submarine industrial base workforce by investing in recruiting, training, and retaining key specialized labor at public and private shipyards; or

children.label: D
children.text: to upgrade facilities, equipment, and infrastructure needed to repair and maintain submarines at public and private shipyards.

children.heading: No further specific authorization in law required
children.label: 2 No further specific authorization in law required
children.text: Funds in the Submarine Security Activities Account may be used as described in this subsection without further specific authorization in law.

children.heading: Plan for use of funds
children.label: d Plan for use of funds

children.text: the amount of funds in the Submarine Security Activities Account; and
how such funds will be used, including specific amounts and purposes.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out subsection (c), the President may transfer funds available in the Submarine Security Activities Account to appropriations available to the Department of Defense.

children.heading: Authority in addition to other transfer authority
children.label: B Authority in addition to other transfer authority
children.text: The authority provided in this paragraph is in addition to any other transfer authority otherwise provided by law and is subject to the same terms and conditions as the authority provided in section 8005 of the Department of Defense Appropriations Act, 2023 (), except for monetary limitations concerning the amount of authority available.Public Law 117–328

children.heading: Availability
children.label: C Availability
children.text: Funds transferred under the authority provided in this paragraph shall be merged with and available for the same purposes, and for the same time period, as the appropriation to which transferred.

children.heading: Limitation on certain transactions
children.label: D Limitation on certain transactions
children.text: Contributions referred to in subsection (a) may not be obligated for a transaction authorized in subsection (c)(1)(B) until the President submits to the appropriate congressional committees and leadership notice of the transaction, including a detailed cost estimate, and a period of 21 days has elapsed after the date on which the notification is received by the appropriate congressional committees and leadership or, if earlier, a period of 14 days has elapsed after the date on which a copy of the notification is provided in an electronic medium.

children.heading: To Department of Energy
children.label: 2 To Department of Energy
children.text: In carrying out subsection (c), and in accordance with the Atomic Energy Act of 1954, ( et seq.), the President may transfer funds available in the Submarine Security Activities Account to appropriations or funds of the Department of Energy available to carry out activities related to AUKUS submarine security activities.42 U.S.C. 2011

children.heading: Transfers back to Submarine Security Activities Account
children.label: 3 Transfers back to Submarine Security Activities Account
children.text: Upon a determination by the President that all or part of the funds transferred from the Submarine Security Activities Account under this subsection are not necessary for the purposes for which such funds were transferred, all or such part of such funds shall be transferred back to the Submarine Security Activities Account.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President may invest money in the Submarine Security Activities Account in securities of the United States or in securities guaranteed as to principal and interest by the United States.

children.heading: Interest
children.label: 2 Interest
children.text: Any interest or other income that accrues from investment in securities referred to in paragraph (1) shall be deposited to the credit of the Submarine Security Activities Account.

children.heading: Relationship to other laws
children.label: g Relationship to other laws
children.text: The authority to accept or transfer funds under this section is in addition to any other statutory authority to accept or transfer funds.

children.label: A
children.text: the intended use of such funds by appropriation, program, project, and activity, as defined in the Joint Explanatory Statement accompanying the Department of Defense Appropriations Act 2023 (); and1Public Law 117–328So in original. Probably should be “Act,”.1

children.label: B
children.text: the extent to which such funds complement, supplement, or supplant other on-going or planned efforts funded by an appropriations Act with an identification of the associated funding and explanation of the combined efforts including the intended outcomes.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of funds transferred under this subsection during the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year in which the report is submitted; and

children.label: B
children.text: the purposes for which such funds were used.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of money so transferred;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the intended use of the funds; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other matters related to the administration of the Submarine Security Activities Account as determined necessary by the Secretary.

children.heading: Form
children.label: 2 Form
children.text: The report required by this subsection shall be submitted in unclassified form but may include a classified annex.
